{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.3,
    "R1.2": 0.7,

    "R1.4": 0.3,
    "R1.5": 0.3,
    "R1.6": 0.7,
    "R1.7": 0.7,
    "RX.1": 1.0,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 1.0,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.2",
    "R1.3",
    "R1.6",
    "R1.7",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": false,
  "clarification_question": "",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "A general request to check the artifact centrally includes assessing whether the argument moves coherently from prior-work gap to dataset, method, results, and conclusion.",
    "R1.3": "The delegation reasonably asks whether the reported numerical results and analyses support the claims made in the summary and contribution statement.",
    "R1.6": "Checking a paper-like passage commonly includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, concision, and academic register.",
    "R1.7": "Because the passage invokes specific prior work and literature positioning, checking it includes responsibility for citation accuracy and scholarly attribution.",
    "RX.1": "Any projection of this broad delegation must preserve uncertainty about what kind of checking was intended.",
    "RX.2": "The checker must avoid endorsing claims such as first-public-release or deployable surrogate beyond what the artifact can support.",
    "RX.3": "The responsibility remains limited to checking the provided research-writing artifact rather than executing edits or external validation.",
    "RX.4": "The passage concerns medicinal-chemistry program decisions, so checking should avoid enabling unsupported deployment claims with downstream scientific or operational harm.",
    "RX.5": "Claims, numbers, and named works should remain traceable to specific statements in the artifact."
  }
}

rationale.R1.2: A general request to check the artifact centrally includes assessing whether the argument moves coherently from prior-work gap to dataset, method, results, and conclusion.
rationale.R1.3: The delegation reasonably asks whether the reported numerical results and analyses support the claims made in the summary and contribution statement.
rationale.R1.6: Checking a paper-like passage commonly includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, concision, and academic register.
rationale.R1.7: Because the passage invokes specific prior work and literature positioning, checking it includes responsibility for citation accuracy and scholarly attribution.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of this broad delegation must preserve uncertainty about what kind of checking was intended.
rationale.RX.2: The checker must avoid endorsing claims such as first-public-release or deployable surrogate beyond what the artifact can support.
rationale.RX.3: The responsibility remains limited to checking the provided research-writing artifact rather than executing edits or external validation.
rationale.RX.4: The passage concerns medicinal-chemistry program decisions, so checking should avoid enabling unsupported deployment claims with downstream scientific or operational harm.
rationale.RX.5: Claims, numbers, and named works should remain traceable to specific statements in the artifact.
